title: Conjecture 1.1 (Alon–Krivelevich)
statement_text: If $G$ is a bipartite graph of maximum degree $\Delta$, then $\operatorname{ch}(G)=O(\log\Delta)$.
context_text: Alon and Krivelevich made this conjecture in 1998, asserting that the lower bound $\operatorname{ch}(G)\geq(1+o(1))\log_2\delta$ of Saxton and Thomason is best possible up to a constant factor for bipartite graphs. The conjecture is still widely open; prior to this work the best known upper bound was $(1+o(1))\frac{\Delta}{\log\Delta}$, which holds for the larger class of triangle-free graphs.
paper_contribution: Proves that every bipartite graph $G$ of sufficiently large maximum degree $\Delta$ satisfies $\operatorname{ch}(G)<(\frac{4}{5}-\varepsilon)\frac{\Delta}{\log\Delta}$ for $\varepsilon=10^{-3}$, giving the first upper bound with leading constant strictly less than $1$ and showing that list coloring behaves fundamentally differently for bipartite graphs than for general triangle-free graphs.
